---
title: "Syncology"
subtitle: "A personal health knowledge graph, and the retrieval research that came out of it"
listing:
  contents:
    - docs/writeup_food_retrieval.qmd
    - docs/writeup_biomarker_resolution.qmd
    - docs/writeup_cycle_inference.qmd
  type: default
  sort: "date desc"
  fields: [title, subtitle, date, categories, reading-time]
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
---

I turned ~4.5 years of my own multi-source, multi-language health data — Apple
Health, lab PDFs, cycle tracking, workouts, a food log — into a clean **DuckDB
warehouse** and a **Kuzu knowledge graph**, with entity resolution across biomarkers
and foods. The infrastructure is the boring, necessary part; the interesting part is
what the data forced me to figure out along the way.

Three of those problems turned into small, rigorous studies — entity resolution in two
opposite regimes (an open cross-lingual vocabulary vs a small closed one), and inferring
a menstrual cycle from irregular data. The through-line is method discipline: put
confidence intervals on everything, match the tool to the regime, and say "unknown" when
the data won't support a call. You can also [**explore the knowledge-graph
schema**](docs/graph_explorer.qmd) interactively — synthetic data, click around.

## Write-ups
